name: Pakistan
headline: Pakistan UPGRADE proposed: 17.2 → 17.6 (+0.4) / Critical band sustained. POSITIVE SIGNAL: PCA Supplemental Award (May 15) upheld Pakistan's stance that IWT remains valid and India cannot unilaterally suspend it — Pakistan won the procedural ruling. Pakistan formally welcomed the award (Radio Pakistan May 17). This is a discrete ACC-dimension positive: Pakistan's active legal engagement at PCA produced a binding-arbitration validation of its treaty position. India's May 17 rejection of the award means the practical effect on Pakistan's water security is UNCHANGED — the suspension continues, 240M civilians remain without IWT-guaranteed flows — so the upward movement is bounded and partial (POSITIVE recognition without structural resolution). Proposed dimensional change: ACC 1.375 → 1.5 (+0.125, recognition of international legal engagement producing a binding favorable ruling). Other dimensions sustained — the underlying humanitarian-vulnerability (240M, 80% agricultural water risk) persists unchanged. Composite: 17.2 → 17.6 / Critical sustained. BOUNDARY STATUS: still 2.4pt below Critical/Developing boundary (20/21) — boundary not approached. The asymmetry between the legal win (positive ACC signal) and the unchanged structural vulnerability (continued EMP exposure) is the methodology-novel pattern: 'upstream-conduct-positive-recognition-without-resolution' — symbolic positive without structural relief. Confidence MEDIUM (clear positive event but partial-recognition principle requires conservative weighting; delta below 5-point threshold = noted-positive-no-change-of-band).

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Awareness of the IWT-suspension structural risk was already documented; the May 17 arbitration outcome is a legal-engagement event.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. The PCA Supplemental Award is a procedural-validation, not a structural change for the 240M downstream civilians. The water-security vulnerability is unchanged. EMP-dimension exposure to externally-imposed risk persists.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Pakistan's UNSC raising, Zardari diplomatic engagement, and PCA case prosecution are procedurally appropriate accountability-mechanism use; pattern was already reflected at prior baseline.

BND.rationale: Sustained. The IWT itself is a boundary-defining international agreement; Pakistan's adherence to legal-engagement framework is consistent with prior baseline.

ACC.rationale: UPGRADE +0.125. AB1 harm-acknowledgment: Pakistan formally raised the IWT suspension at PCA arbitration and at UNSC; the May 15 PCA Supplemental Award is binding-arbitration validation of Pakistan's accountability-mechanism engagement. AB4 procedural-engagement: Pakistan pursued international legal remedies through established channels (PCA, UNSC) and produced a binding favorable ruling. The upward signal is bounded by the partial-recognition principle — the structural water-security situation is unchanged because India rejected the ruling. Eighth-step (0.125) upgrade reflects the discrete positive event without overweighting an outcome that produces no structural relief for affected populations.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. The IWT-suspension structural water-security risk (80%+ agricultural water at risk; no alternative systems) is unchanged — the legal win does not restore water flows.

INT.rationale: Sustained. Pakistan's INT-dimension scoring relates to internal-governance consistency; the IWT arbitration event is an external-engagement positive that does not bear on internal-consistency scoring.

evidence.summary: Radio Pakistan May 17: Pakistan welcomes Court of Arbitration's Supplemental Award on Indus Waters Treaty. Foreign Office spokesperson confirmed continued engagement with international legal frameworks.

Daily Pakistan May 17: Pakistan wins key procedural ruling against India in Indus Waters Treaty arbitration case. PCA Supplemental Award (May 15) upheld Pakistan's stance that IWT remains valid and India cannot unilaterally suspend it.
Tribune (Pakistan): arbitration court upholds Pakistan's stance on Indus Waters Treaty. External corroboration of Pakistan's legal-engagement win.

India rejected the award the same day (May 17) as 'null and void' — the practical effect on Pakistan's water security is UNCHANGED (suspension continues; 240M civilians remain without IWT-guaranteed flows).

Chatham House April 2026: 'India and Pakistan still cannot agree to restore the Indus Waters Treaty' — no restoration pathway active despite the favorable ruling. Confirms partial-recognition principle.

keyEvidence: May 17: Pakistan formally welcomed PCA Supplemental Award (May 15) upholding IWT validity and ruling India cannot unilaterally suspend it.
Pakistan pursued international legal remedies through established channels (PCA, UNSC) producing binding favorable ruling.
India rejected the ruling the same day ('null and void'); practical water-security situation unchanged.
240M downstream civilians remain dependent on IWT-governed flows; 80%+ agricultural water risk persists.
Proposed dimensional change: ACC 1.375 → 1.5 (+0.125, partial-recognition principle) → composite 17.2 → 17.6 / Critical sustained.
BOUNDARY STATUS: 2.4pt below Critical/Developing boundary (20/21); boundary not approached this cycle.
Methodology-novel: 'upstream-conduct-positive-recognition-without-resolution' pattern (symbolic positive without structural relief).
rationale: Proposed +0.4 composite change reflects a discrete May 17 ACC-dimension positive event (binding-arbitration ruling in Pakistan's favor) appropriately bounded by the partial-recognition principle. Pakistan's active legal engagement at PCA produced a binding favorable validation of its treaty position — this is recognition of accountability-mechanism use. However, India's same-day rejection of the ruling means the structural water-security situation for 240M civilians is unchanged. The upward movement is therefore bounded and partial: an eighth-step ACC upgrade (1.375 → 1.5) acknowledges the discrete positive event without overweighting an outcome that produces no structural relief. The proposed composite 17.6 keeps Pakistan within the Critical band, still 2.4pt below the Critical/Developing boundary — boundary crossing is not approached this cycle. Math: (1.875+1.875+1.875+1.375+1.875+1.5+1.875+1.375)/8 = 13.625/8 = 1.703125 → ((1.703125-1)/4)*100 = 17.578125 → 17.6. Note: +0.4 delta is below the 5-point change-proposal threshold — flagging as 'noted-positive-no-band-change' rather than a formal upgrade proposal eligible for auto-application. Human reviewer should decide whether to apply or hold per sub-threshold protocol.

India response to the PCA ruling beyond the May 17 rejection — pattern test
Agricultural water-allocation system stress (80%+ at risk; first measurable impact)
Monsoon season forward window: next NDMA forecast cycle
Boundary status: 17.6 sits 2.4pt below 20/21 — boundary not approached
carryForward: IWT suspension as structural water-security risk (forward SYS watch)
240M downstream civilians dependent on IWT-governed water flows
Chatham House April 2026 'no restoration pathway' finding
NEW: legal-defiance-arc cross-entity coupled event (India + Pakistan IWT bifurcation)
newConductCategoryNote: PROPOSED NEW METHODOLOGY CATEGORY: 'upstream-conduct-positive-recognition-without-resolution' — when a downstream/harmed-party state wins a binding international legal ruling against an upstream/harming-party state but the upstream party formally rejects the ruling, the downstream state receives a procedural-ACC-positive but no structural EMP/SYS relief. Pakistan May 17 is the canonical instance: PCA ruled in Pakistan's favor; India rejected the ruling; 240M civilians remain without IWT-governed water. The methodology should formalize that partial recognition (procedural win without structural relief) produces a bounded upgrade — an eighth-step on the ACC dimension, not a full half-step — to avoid overweighting symbolic outcomes that do not produce measurable population-level benefit. Coupled with 'legal-defiance-arc' (India + Pakistan IWT bifurcation), this is the first cross-entity legal-event coupling in the country index.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Proposed composite 17.6 sits 2.4pt below Critical/Developing boundary (20/21). Critical band sustained. Boundary not approached this cycle.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. +0.4 composite delta from ACC +0.125 single-dimension upgrade. Critical band sustained with 2.4pt below 20/21 boundary. NOTE: +0.4 is below the 5pt change-proposal threshold — flagged as noted-positive-no-band-change; human reviewer decides on sub-threshold application.
